title: Statue of Liberty is created
subtitle: American icon in New York, United States
fact: The Statue of Liberty is a colossal neoclassical sculpture of a robed and crowned woman on Liberty Island in New York Harbor, within New York City, U.S.

title: Christ the Redeemer is created
subtitle: Colossal statue of Jesus in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil
fact: Christ the Redeemer is an Art Deco statue of Jesus in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, created by French-Polish sculptor Paul Landowski and built by Brazilian engineer Heitor da Silva Costa, in collaboration with French engineer Albert Caquot.

title: David is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Michelangelo
fact: David is a masterpiece of Italian Renaissance sculpture in marble created from 1501 to 1504 by Michelangelo.

title: Venus de Milo is created
subtitle: Ancient Greek marble statue of a woman
fact: The Venus de Milo or Aphrodite of Melos is an ancient Greek marble sculpture that was created during the Hellenistic period.

title: Pietà (Michelangelo) is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Michelangelo
fact: The Pietà is a Carrara marble sculpture of Jesus and Mary at Mount Golgotha representing the "Sixth Sorrow" of the Virgin Mary by Michelangelo Buonarroti, in Saint Peter's Basilica, Vatican City, for which it was made.

title: Statue of Unity is created
subtitle: Statue of Vallabhbhai Patel at the bank of Narmada river, Gujarat, India
fact: The Statue of Unity is the world's tallest statue, with a height of 182 metres, located in Narmada valley, near Kevadia in the state of Gujarat, India.

title: Winged Victory of Samothrace is created
subtitle: Statue from Samothrace, Greece in the Louvre, Paris, France
fact: The Winged Victory of Samothrace, or the Nike of Samothrace, is a votive monument originally discovered on the island of Samothrace in the northeastern Aegean Sea.

title: The Motherland Calls is created
subtitle: Volgograd monumental sculpture for heroes of the Battle of Stalingrad
fact: The Motherland Calls is a colossal neoclassicist and socialist realist war memorial sculpture on Mamayev Kurgan in Volgograd, Russia.

title: Cloud Gate is created
subtitle: Public art sculpture in Chicago, Illinois, United States
fact: Cloud Gate is a public sculpture by artist Anish Kapoor, that is the centerpiece of Grainger Plaza at Millennium Park in the Loop community area of Chicago, Illinois, United States.

title: Comedian is created
subtitle: Maurizio Cattelan artwork
fact: Comedian is a 2019 artwork by the Italian artist Maurizio Cattelan.

title: Ecstasy of Saint Teresa is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Gian Lorenzo Bernini
fact: The Ecstasy of Saint Teresa is a sculptural altarpiece group in white marble set in an elevated aedicule in the Cornaro Chapel of the church of Santa Maria della Vittoria in Rome.

title: Le génie du mal is created
subtitle: Religious sculpture by Belgian artist Guillaume Geefs
fact: Le génie du mal, known informally in English as Lucifer or The Lucifer of Liège is a religious sculpture executed in white marble and installed in 1848 by the Belgian artist Guillaume Geefs.

title: Shoes on the Danube Bank is created
subtitle: Memorial in Budapest, Hungary
fact: The Shoes on the Danube Bank is a memorial erected on 16 April 2005, in Budapest, Hungary.

title: Charging Bull is created
subtitle: Bronze sculpture in New York
fact: Charging Bull is a bronze sculpture that stands on Broadway just north of Bowling Green in the Financial District of Manhattan in New York City.

title: The Rape of Proserpina is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Gian Lorenzo Bernini
fact: The Rape of Proserpina, more accurately translated as The Abduction of Proserpina, is a large Baroque marble group sculpture by Italian artist Gian Lorenzo Bernini, executed in 1621 and '22, when Bernini's career was in its early stage.

title: Vessel is created
subtitle: Structure in New York City
fact: Vessel is a structure and visitor attraction built as part of Hudson Yards in Manhattan, New York City, New York.

title: Laocoön and His Sons is created
subtitle: Sculpture in the Museo Pio-Clementino
fact: The statue of Laocoön and His Sons, also called the Laocoön Group, has been one of the most famous ancient sculptures since it was excavated in Rome in 1506 and put on public display in the Vatican Museums, where it remains today.

title: Kryptos is created
subtitle: Encrypted sculpture by American artist Jim Sanborn
fact: Kryptos is a sculpture by the American artist Jim Sanborn located on the grounds of the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) headquarters, the George Bush Center for Intelligence in Langley, Virginia.

title: Angel of the North is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Antony Gormley
fact: The Angel of the North is a contemporary sculpture by Antony Gormley, located in Gateshead, Tyne and Wear, England.

title: Discobolus is created
subtitle: Lost sculpture by Myron known through several copies
fact: The Discobolus by Myron is an ancient Greek sculpture completed at the start of the Classical period in around 460–450 BC that depicts an ancient Greek athlete throwing a discus.

title: Augustus of Prima Porta is created
subtitle: Ancient Roman sculpture of the emperor Augustus
fact: The Augustus of Prima Porta is a full-length portrait statue of Augustus, the first Roman emperor.

title: Moses is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Michelangelo
fact: Moses is a sculpture by the Italian High Renaissance artist Michelangelo, housed in the Basilica of San Pietro in Vincoli in Rome.

title: The Kelpies is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Andy Scott
fact: The Kelpies are a pair of monumental steel horse-heads between the Scottish towns of Falkirk and Grangemouth.

title: David is created
subtitle: Bronze statue by Donatello, Museo nazionale del Bargello
fact: David is a bronze statue of the biblical hero by the Italian Early Renaissance sculptor Donatello, probably made in the 1440s, and now in the Bargello, Florence.

title: Bust of Queen Nefertiti is created
subtitle: Bust of Queen Nefertiti from the Amarna Period in the Neues Museum, Berlin
fact: The Nefertiti Bust is a painted stucco-coated limestone bust of Nefertiti, the Great Royal Wife of Egyptian pharaoh Akhenaten.

title: Madonna of Bruges is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Michelangelo
fact: The Madonna of Bruges is a marble sculpture by Michelangelo of the Virgin and Child.

title: Merlion is created
subtitle: Fountain statue in Singapore
fact: The Merlion is the official mascot of Singapore.

title: Lion Capital of Asoka is created
subtitle: Capital of a column of Mauryan emperor Ashoka in Sarnath, Uttar Pradesh, India
fact: The Lion Capital of Ashoka is the capital, or head, of a column erected by the Mauryan emperor Ashoka in Sarnath, India, c.

title: Fearless Girl is created
subtitle: Bronze statue in Manhattan
fact: Fearless Girl is a bronze sculpture by Kristen Visbal of a 4-foot-high (1.2 m) girl standing in a self-confident pose.

title: Big Vishnu is created
subtitle: 75-Meter tall statue located in Garuda Wisnu Kencana Cultural Park
fact: Garuda Wisnu Kencana statue is a 121-meter-tall statue located in Garuda Wisnu Kencana Cultural Park, Bali, Indonesia.

title: Spire of Dublin is created
subtitle: Monument in Dublin, Ireland
fact: The Spire of Dublin, alternatively titled the Millennium Spire or the Monument of Light, is a large, stainless steel, pin-like monument 120 metres (390 ft) in height, located on the site of the former Nelson's Pillar on O'Connell Street, the main thoroughfare of Dublin, Ireland.

title: Cadillac Ranch is created
subtitle: Public art installation and sculpture in Amarillo, Texas
fact: Cadillac Ranch is a public art installation and sculpture in Amarillo, Texas, US.

title: Maman is created
subtitle: Series of sculptures by Louise Bourgeois
fact: Maman (1999) is a bronze, stainless steel, and marble sculpture in several locations by the artist Louise Bourgeois.

title: Dying Gaul is created
subtitle: Sculpture
fact: The Dying Gaul, also called The Dying Galatian or The Dying Gladiator, is an ancient Roman marble semi-recumbent statue now in the Capitoline Museums in Rome.

title: Santo Niño de Cebú is created
subtitle: Cebuano title of Jesus Christ as an infant
fact: The Señor Santo Niño de Cebú, or simply the Santo Niño, is a Catholic title of the Child Jesus associated with a religious image widely venerated as miraculous by Filipino Catholics.

title: Heirloom Seal of the Realm is created
subtitle: Chinese seal carved from jade
fact: The Heirloom Seal of the Realm, also known in English as the Imperial Seal of China, was a Chinese jade seal allegedly carved out of the Heshibi, a sacred piece of jade.

title: Venus of Hohle Fels is created
subtitle: Sculpture
fact: The Venus of Hohle Fels is an Upper Paleolithic Venus figurine made of mammoth ivory that was unearthed in 2008 in Hohle Fels, a cave near Schelklingen, Germany, part of the Caves and Ice Age Art in the Swabian Jura UNESCO World Heritage Site.

title: Apollo and Daphne is created
subtitle: Marble sculpture by Gian Lorenzo Bernini
fact: Apollo and Daphne is a life-sized marble sculpture by the Italian artist Gian Lorenzo Bernini, which was executed between 1622 and 1625.

title: The Sphere is created
subtitle: Sculpture in New York City
fact: The Sphere is a monumental cast bronze sculpture by German artist Fritz Koenig.

title: Aztec sun stone is created
subtitle: 16Th century Mexica sculpture
fact: The Aztec sun stone is a late post-classic Mexica sculpture housed in the National Anthropology Museum in Mexico City, and is perhaps the most famous work of Mexica sculpture.

title: Sleeping Hermaphrodite is created
subtitle: Ancient marble sculpture, modified by Gianlorenzo Bernini
fact: Sleeping Hermaphroditus or Sleeping Hermaphrodite is an ancient Roman marble sculpture depicting Hermaphroditus life size; it rests on a marble mattress completed by Italian artist Gian Lorenzo Bernini in 1620.

title: Perseus with the Head of Medusa is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Benvenuto Cellini
fact: Perseus with the Head of Medusa is a bronze sculpture made by Benvenuto Cellini in the period 1545–1554.

title: Lion Monument is created
subtitle: Memorial in city of Lucerne, Switzerland
fact: The Lion Monument, or the Lion of Lucerne, is a rock relief in Lucerne, Switzerland, designed by Bertel Thorvaldsen and hewn in 1820–21 by Lukas Ahorn.

title: Statue of Freedom is created
subtitle: Artwork by Thomas Crawford, statue on top of the US Capitol
fact: The Statue of Freedom, also known as Armed Freedom or simply Freedom, is a bronze statue designed by Thomas Crawford that, since 1863, has crowned the United States Capitol dome.

title: Gommateshwara statue is created
subtitle: Jain sculpture in Shravanbelagola, India
fact: The Gommateshwara statue is a 57-foot (17 m) high monolithic statue on Vindhyagiri, also known as Indra-giri in the town of Shravanbelagola in the Indian state of Karnataka.

title: Spiral Jetty is created
subtitle: Earthwork sculpture by Robert Smithson in Box Elder County, Utah, United States
fact: Spiral Jetty is a work of land art constructed in April 1970 that is considered to be the most important work by American sculptor Robert Smithson.

title: Tian Tan Buddha is created
subtitle: The second largest outdoor bronze Buddha in the world
fact: The Big Buddha is a large bronze sculpture of Buddha, completed in 1993, and located at Ngong Ping, near Po Lin Monastery on Lantau Island, Hong Kong.

title: Veiled Christ is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Giuseppe Sanmartino
fact: Veiled Christ is a carved marble tomb effigy completed in 1753 by the Neapolitan artist Giuseppe Sanmartino.

title: Statue of Hachikō is created
subtitle: Statue in Shibuya, Tokyo
fact: A statue of the Akita dog Hachikō, remembered for his unwavering loyalty to his deceased owner, is installed outside Tokyo's Shibuya Station, in Japan.

title: Tusculum portrait is created
subtitle: Roman bust, only extant portrait of Julius Caesar made during his lifetime
fact: The Tusculum portrait, also called the Tusculum bust, is the only extant sculpture of Julius Caesar which may have been made during his lifetime.

title: Venus Callipyge is created
subtitle: Type of antique Venus
fact: The Venus Callipyge, also known as the Aphrodite Kallipygos or the Callipygian Venus, all literally meaning "Venus of the beautiful buttocks", is an Ancient Roman marble statue, thought to be a copy of an older Greek original.

title: Unisphere is created
subtitle: Steel structure in Queens, New York
fact: The Unisphere is a spherical stainless steel representation of the Earth at Flushing Meadows–Corona Park in Queens, New York City, New York.

title: Farnese Hercules is created
subtitle: Ancient statue of Hercules in the National Archaeological Museum, Naples
fact: The Farnese Hercules is an ancient statue of Hercules made in the early third century AD and signed by Glykon, who is otherwise unknown; he was an Athenian but he may have worked in Rome.

title: Psyche Revived by Cupid's Kiss is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Antonio Canova
fact: Psyche Revived by Cupid's Kiss is a sculpture by Italian artist Antonio Canova first commissioned in 1787 by Colonel John Campbell.

title: The Kiss is created
subtitle: Marble sculpture by the French sculptor Auguste Rodin
fact: The Kiss is an 1882 marble sculpture by the French sculptor Auguste Rodin.

title: Victory Arch is created
subtitle: Pair of triumphal arches in central Baghdad, Iraq
fact: The Victory Arch, officially known as the Swords of Qādisīyah, and popularly called the Hands of Victory or the Crossed Swords, are a pair of triumphal arches in central Baghdad, Iraq.

title: Emerald Buddha is created
subtitle: Statue considered the Palladium of Thailand
fact: The Emerald Buddha is an image of the meditating Gautama Buddha seated in a meditative posture, made of a semi-precious green stone, clothed in gold, and about 66 centimetres (26 in) tall.

title: Apollo Belvedere is created
subtitle: Roman statue copy after a Greek bronze original attributed to Leochares
fact: The Apollo Belvedere is a celebrated marble sculpture from classical antiquity.

title: St. Peter's Baldachin is created
subtitle: Sculptural monument by Gian Lorenzo Bernini
fact: St.

title: Doryphoros is created
subtitle: Lost statue by Polykleitos / type of statue
fact: The Doryphoros of Polykleitos is one of the best known Greek sculptures of Classical antiquity, depicting a solidly built, muscular, standing warrior, originally bearing a spear balanced on his left shoulder.

title: For the Love of God is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Damien Hirst
fact: For the Love of God is a sculpture by artist Damien Hirst produced in 2007.

title: Equestrian statue of the Duke of Wellington is created
subtitle: Statue by Carlo Marochetti in Glasgow, United Kingdom
fact: The equestrian statue of Field Marshal Arthur Wellesley, 1st Duke of Wellington, is located outside the Gallery of Modern Art, Glasgow in Scotland.

title: Chair of Saint Peter is created
subtitle: Artwork / throne in St Peter's, Rome
fact: The Chair of Saint Peter, also known as the Throne of Saint Peter, is a relic conserved in St.

title: David is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Gian Lorenzo Bernini
fact: David is a life-size marble sculpture by Gian Lorenzo Bernini.

title: Fallen Astronaut is created
subtitle: Sculpture of an astronaut on the moon
fact: Fallen Astronaut is a 3.5-inch (8.9 cm) aluminum sculpture created by Belgian artist Paul Van Hoeydonck.

title: Riace bronzes is created
subtitle: Two ancient Greek sculptures
fact: The Riace bronzes, also called the Riace Warriors, are two full-size Greek bronze statues of bearded warriors, cast about 460–450 BC that were found in the sea in 1972 near Riace, Calabria, in southern Italy.

title: Statue of Abraham Lincoln is created
subtitle: Statue at the Lincoln Memorial, Washington, D.C.
fact: Abraham Lincoln (1920) is a colossal seated figure of the 16th president of the United States, Abraham Lincoln (1809–1865), sculpted by Daniel Chester French (1850–1931) and carved by the Piccirilli Brothers.

title: Monument to Dante is created
subtitle: Statue outside Santa Croce, Florence
fact: The Statue of Dante Alighieri is a monument to Dante Alighieri in Piazza Santa Croce, outside the Basilica of Santa Croce, in Florence, Italy.

title: Lady of Elche is created
subtitle: Iberian sculpture
fact: The Lady of Elche is a limestone bust that was discovered in 1897 in La Alcudia, an archaeological site on a private estate two kilometers south of Elche, Spain.

title: Statue of the Bear and the Strawberry Tree is created
subtitle: Public sculpture in Madrid
fact: The Statue of the Bear and the Strawberry Tree is a sculpture from the second half of the 20th century, situated in the Spanish capital, Madrid.

title: The Bride Stripped Bare By Her Bachelors, Even is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Marcel Duchamp
fact: The Bride Stripped Bare by Her Bachelors, Even, also called The Large Glass, is an artwork by Marcel Duchamp over 9 feet (2.7 m) tall and almost 6 feet (1.76m) wide.

title: Alexander Sarcophagus is created
subtitle: Ancient sarcophagus
fact: The Alexander Sarcophagus is a late 4th century BC Hellenistic stone sarcophagus from the Royal necropolis of Ayaa near Sidon, Lebanon.

title: ArcelorMittal Orbit is created
subtitle: Sculpture and observation tower in the Olympic Park in Stratford, London
fact: The ArcelorMittal Orbit is a 114.5-metre (376-foot) sculpture and observation tower in Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park in Stratford, London.

title: Columbus Circle is created
subtitle: Monument, circle and neighborhood in Manhattan, New York City
fact: Columbus Circle is a traffic circle and heavily trafficked intersection in the New York City borough of Manhattan, located at the intersection of Eighth Avenue, Broadway, Central Park South, and Central Park West, at the southwest corner of Central Park.

title: Kindlifresserbrunnen is created
subtitle: 16Th century fountain located at the Kornhausplatz, in Bern, Switzerland
fact: The Kindlifresserbrunnen is a painted stone fountain at the Kornhausplatz in Bern, Switzerland.

title: Étant donnés is created
subtitle: Artwork by Marcel Duchamp
fact: Étant donnés is a 1966 assemblage by Marcel Duchamp.

title: Lobster Telephone is created
subtitle: Surrealist art sculpture
fact: Lobster Telephone is a Surrealist object, created by Salvador Dalí in 1936 for the English poet Edward James (1907–1984), a leading collector of surrealist art.

title: Bird in Space is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Constantin Brâncuși
fact: Bird in Space is a series of sculptures by Romanian sculptor Constantin Brâncuși.

title: Statue of Equality is created
subtitle: Statue of Sri Ramanuja in Telangana, India
fact: The Statue of Equality is a statue of the 11th-century Indian philosopher Ramanuja, located on the premises of the Chinna Jeeyar Trust at Muchintal, Ranga Reddy district in the outskirts of Hyderabad.

title: Muisca raft is created
subtitle: Pre-Columbian votive piece
fact: The Muisca raft, sometimes referred to as the Golden Raft of El Dorado or the Pasca raft, is a pre-Columbian votive piece created by the Muisca, an Andean people of Colombia in the Eastern Ranges of the Colombian Andes that established multiple chiefdoms in the region.

title: Statue of Peace is created
subtitle: Memorial statue
fact: The Statue of Peace, often shortened to Sonyeosang in Korean or Shōjo-zō in Japanese and sometimes called the Comfort Woman Statue, is a symbol of the victims of sexual slavery, known euphemistically as comfort women, by the Japanese military during World War II, specifically, the period from the beginning of the Second Sino-Japanese War until the end of the Pacific War.

title: Hermannsdenkmal is created
subtitle: Monument in North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany
fact: The Hermann Memorial is a monument located southwest of Detmold in the district of Lippe, in Germany.

title: Stele of the Vultures is created
subtitle: Historical and mythical limestone account / depiction
fact: The Stele of the Vultures is a monument from the Early Dynastic IIIb period in Mesopotamia celebrating a victory of the city-state of Lagash over its neighbour Umma in the Umma–Lagash war.

title: Jeanneke Pis is created
subtitle: Bronze sculpture and fountain by Denis-Adrien Debouvrie in Brussels, Belgium
fact: Jeanneke Pis is a modern fountain sculpture in central Brussels, Belgium.

title: Oscar Wilde's tomb is created
subtitle: Tomb of Oscar Wilde
fact: Oscar Wilde's tomb is located in Père Lachaise Cemetery, Paris, France.

title: Jesus the Homeless is created
subtitle: Sculpture depicting Jesus as a homeless person
fact: Homeless Jesus, also known as Jesus the Homeless, is a bronze sculpture by Timothy Schmalz depicting Jesus as a homeless person, sleeping on a park bench.

title: Kindred Spirits is created
subtitle: Outdoor sculpture in County Cork, Ireland
fact: Kindred Spirits is a large stainless steel outdoor sculpture in Bailick Park in Midleton, County Cork, Ireland.

title: Love is created
subtitle: Pop art image by Robert Indiana
fact: Love is a pop art image by American artist Robert Indiana.

title: Serpent Column is created
subtitle: Victory column in Istanbul, Turkey, from Delphi
fact: The Serpent Column, also known as the Serpentine Column, Plataean Tripod or Delphi Tripod, is an ancient bronze column at the Hippodrome of Constantinople in what is now Istanbul, Turkey.

title: Rape of the Sabine Women is created
subtitle: Marble sculpture by Giambologna, Loggia dei Lanzi
fact: Abduction of a Sabine Woman is a large and complex marble statue by the Flemish sculptor and architect Giambologna.

title: Dendera zodiac is created
subtitle: Ancient Egyptian artifact of carved sandstone, now in the Louvre
fact: The sculptured Dendera zodiac is a widely known Egyptian bas-relief from the ceiling of the pronaos of a chapel dedicated to Osiris in the Hathor temple at Dendera, containing images of Taurus and Libra.

title: Head of Franz Kafka is created
subtitle: Sculpture in Prague, Czech Republic
fact: The Head of Franz Kafka, also known as the Statue of Kafka, is an outdoor kinetic sculpture by David Černý depicting Czech German-language writer Franz Kafka, installed on 31 October 2014 outside of the Quadrio shopping mall in Prague, Czech Republic.

title: Kyffhäuser Monument is created
subtitle: Monument
fact: The Kyffhäuser Monument, also known as Barbarossa Monument, is an Emperor William monument in the Kyffhäuser mountain range in the German state of Thuringia.

title: Parthenon Frieze is created
subtitle: Sculpture from the Parthenon, in the Athenian Acropolis
fact: The Parthenon Frieze is the low-relief Pentelic marble sculpture created to adorn the upper part of the Parthenon's naos.

title: Bacchus is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Michelangelo
fact: Bacchus (1496–1497) is a marble sculpture by the Italian High Renaissance sculptor, painter, architect and poet Michelangelo.

title: Statue of Edward Colston is created
subtitle: Former public statue in Bristol, England
fact: The statue of Edward Colston is a bronze statue of Bristol-born merchant and transatlantic slave trader Edward Colston (1636–1721).

title: Another Place is created
subtitle: Installation artwork by Antony Gormley
fact: Another Place is a piece of modern sculpture by British artist Antony Gormley located at Crosby Beach in Merseyside, England.

title: Christus is created
subtitle: Statue by Bertel Thorvaldsen in Church of Our Lady in Copenhagen
fact: Christus is an 1833 white Carrara marble statue of the resurrected Jesus by Bertel Thorvaldsen located in the Church of Our Lady, an Evangelical Lutheran Church of Denmark in Copenhagen, Denmark.

title: Mother of All Asia–Tower of Peace is created
subtitle: Statue of the Virgin Mary in Batangas City
fact: The Mother of All Asia–Tower of Peace is a monument shrine dedicated to the Virgin Mary as a symbol of unity and peace in Asia and the whole world.

title: One and Three Chairs is created
subtitle: Conceptual artwork by Joseph Kosuth
fact: One and Three Chairs, is a conceptual work by Joseph Kosuth, from 1965.

title: Venus de' Medici is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Cleomenes the Athenian
fact: The Venus de' Medici or Medici Venus is a 1.53 m tall Hellenistic marble sculpture depicting the Greek goddess of love Aphrodite.

title: Unique Forms of Continuity in Space is created
subtitle: Sculpture with multiple casts by Umberto Boccioni
fact: Unique Forms of Continuity in Space is a 1913 bronze Futurist sculpture by Umberto Boccioni.

title: Statue of Lenin, Seattle is created
subtitle: Sculpture
fact: The Statue of Lenin is a 16 ft (5 m) bronze statue of Russian communist revolutionary Vladimir Lenin in the Fremont neighborhood of Seattle, Washington, United States.

title: Atlas is created
subtitle: Art Deco statue of Atlas at the Rockefeller Center, New York City, U.S.A.
fact: Atlas is a bronze statue in Rockefeller Center, within the International Building's courtyard, in Midtown Manhattan in New York City.

title: Venus of Brassempouy is created
subtitle: Sculpture, Paleolithic carved head
fact: The Venus of Brassempouy is a fragmentary ivory figurine from the Upper Palaeolithic, apparently broken from a larger figure at some time unknown.

title: Artemision Bronze is created
subtitle: Ancient Greek sculpture
fact: The Artemision Bronze is an ancient Greek sculpture that was recovered from the sea off Cape Artemision, in northern Euboea, Greece.

title: Franks Casket is created
subtitle: Anglo-Saxon carved chest
fact: The Franks Casket is a small Anglo-Saxon whale's bone chest from the early 8th century, now in the British Museum.

title: The Deposition is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Michelangelo
fact: The Deposition is a marble sculpture by the Italian High Renaissance master Michelangelo.

title: Barberini Faun is created
subtitle: Sculpture
fact: The life-size ancient but much restored marble statue known as the Barberini Faun, Fauno Barberini or Drunken Satyr is now in the Glyptothek in Munich, Germany.

title: Lion of Venice is created
subtitle: Statue on St. Mark's Square, Venice, Italy
fact: The Lion of Venice is an ancient bronze sculpture of a winged lion in the Piazzetta di San Marco, in the Italian city of Venice, which came to symbolize the city—as well as one of its patron saints, Saint Mark—after its arrival there in the 12th century.

title: Emancipation Memorial is created
subtitle: Artwork by Thomas Ball
fact: The Emancipation Memorial, also known as the Freedman's Memorial or the Emancipation Group is a monument in Lincoln Park in the Capitol Hill neighborhood of Washington, D.C.

title: Farnese Atlas is created
subtitle: Ancient Roman statue of Greek Deity
fact: The Farnese Atlas is a 2nd-century CE Roman marble sculpture of Atlas holding up a celestial globe.

title: Kritios Boy is created
subtitle: Ancient Greek sculpture from the Acropolis of Athens
fact: The marble Kritios Boy or Kritian Boy belongs to the Early Classical period of ancient Greek sculpture.

title: Headington Shark is created
subtitle: Sculpture in Oxford, England, UK
fact: The Headington Shark is a rooftop sculpture located at 2 New High Street, Headington, Oxford, England, depicting a large shark embedded head-first in the roof of a house.

title: Penitent Magdalene is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Donatello
fact: The Penitent Magdalene is a wooden sculpture of Mary Magdalene by the Italian Renaissance sculptor Donatello, now usually dated to around 1440.

title: Trundholm Sun Chariot is created
subtitle: Late Nordic Bronze Age artifact discovered in Denmark
fact: The Trundholm sun chariot is a Nordic Bronze Age artifact discovered in Denmark.

title: The Seated Scribe is created
subtitle: Statue depicting a scribe with crossed legs and writing tools ready
fact: The sculpture of the Seated Scribe or Squatting Scribe is a famous work of ancient Egyptian art.

title: Battersea Shield is created
subtitle: Celtic archaeological discovery
fact: The Battersea Shield is one of the most significant pieces of ancient Celtic art found in Britain.

title: Diana of Versailles is created
subtitle: Roman sculpture of Artemis after a lost Greek bronze original by Leochares
fact: The Diana of Versailles or Artemis, Goddess of the Hunt is a slightly over-lifesize marble statue of the Roman goddess Diana with a deer.

title: Sarcophagus of the Spouses is created
subtitle: Etruscan sarcophagus in the National Etruscan Museum
fact: The Sarcophagus of the Spouses is a tomb effigy considered one of the masterpieces of Etruscan art.

title: Charioteer of Delphi is created
subtitle: Sculpture
fact: The Charioteer of Delphi, also known as Heniokhos, is an ancient Greek bronze statue dating to around 470 BC.

title: Madurodam is created
subtitle: Miniature park in The Hague, the Netherlands
fact: Madurodam is a miniature park and tourist attraction in the Scheveningen district of The Hague in the Netherlands.

title: Judith and Holofernes is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Donatello
fact: Judith and Holofernes (1457–1464) is a bronze sculpture created by the Italian Renaissance sculptor Donatello towards the end of his life and career.

title: Venus of Tan-Tan is created
subtitle: Sculpture
fact: The Venus of Tan-Tan is an alleged artifact found in Morocco.

title: Piraeus Lion is created
subtitle: Ancient sculpted lion now in Venice arsenal
fact: The Piraeus Lion is one of four lion statues on display at the Venetian Arsenal, Italy, where it was displayed as a symbol of Venice's patron saint, Saint Mark.

title: Saint Longinus is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Gianlorenzo Bernini
fact: Saint Longinus is a sculpture by the Italian artist Gian Lorenzo Bernini.

title: Apennine Colossus is created
subtitle: Statue in Tuscany, Italy
fact: The Apennine Colossus is a stone statue, approximately 11 meters high, in the estate of the Villa Demidoff in Vaglia, Tuscany, Italy.

title: Hermes and the Infant Dionysus is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Praxiteles found at Olympia, Greece
fact: Hermes and the Infant Dionysus, also known as the Hermes of Praxiteles or the Hermes of Olympia is an ancient Greek sculpture of Hermes and the infant Dionysus discovered in 1877 in the ruins of the Temple of Hera, Olympia, in Greece.

title: Minute man is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Daniel Chester French
fact: The Minute Man is an 1874 sculpture by Daniel Chester French in Minute Man National Historical Park, located in Concord, Massachusetts, United States.

title: Wikipedia Monument is created
subtitle: Sculpture in Poland
fact: The Wikipedia Monument, located in Słubice, Poland, is a statue designed by Armenian sculptor Mihran Hakobyan honoring Wikipedia contributors.

title: Cellini Salt Cellar is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Benvenuto Cellini, Kunsthistorisches Museum Wien
fact: The Cellini Salt Cellar is a part-enamelled gold table sculpture by Benvenuto Cellini (c.1500–1571).

title: Stalin Monument is created
subtitle: Sculpture in Prague, Czech Republic
fact: Stalin's Monument was a 15.5 m (51 ft) granite statue honoring Joseph Stalin in Prague, Czechoslovakia.

title: Saqqara Bird is created
subtitle: Bird-shaped artifact made of sycamore wood
fact: The Saqqara Bird is a model bird made of sycamore wood and mounted on a stick, discovered during the 1898 excavation of the tomb of Pa-di-Imen in Saqqara, Egypt.

title: Ludovisi Gaul is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Epigonus of Pergamum
fact: The Ludovisi Gaul is an ancient Roman statue depicting a Gallic man plunging a sword into his breast as he holds up the dead body of his wife.

title: Nike of Paionios is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Paeonius of Mende
fact: The Nike of Paionios is an ancient statue of the Greek goddess of victory, Nike, made by sculptor Paionios between 425 BC and 420 BC.

title: Elephant and Obelisk is created
subtitle: Sculpture designed by Gianlorenzo Bernini
fact: Elephant and Obelisk is a statue of an elephant carrying an obelisk, designed by the Italian artist Gian Lorenzo Bernini.

title: Rebellious Slave is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Michelangelo
fact: The Rebellious Slave is a 2.15m high marble statue by Michelangelo, dated to 1513.

title: Saint George is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Donatello
fact: Saint George is a marble sculpture by Donatello.

title: Aeneas, Anchises and Ascanius is created
subtitle: Sculpture group by Gianlorenzo Bernini
fact: Aeneas, Anchises, and Ascanius is a sculpture by the Italian artist Gian Lorenzo Bernini created c.

title: Wawel Dragon (statue) is created
subtitle: Statue in Kraków, Poland
fact: Wawel Dragon Statue is a monument at the foot of the Wawel Hill in Kraków, Poland, in front of the Wawel Dragon's den, dedicated to the mythical Wawel Dragon.

title: Buddha Dordenma statue is created
subtitle: Statue in Bhutan
fact: Great Buddha Dordenma is a gigantic Shakyamuni Buddha statue in the mountains of Bhutan celebrating the 60th anniversary of fourth king Jigme Singye Wangchuck.

title: Sea organ is created
subtitle: Architectural sound art object located in Zadar, Croatia
fact: The Sea organ is an architectural sound art object located in Zadar, Croatia and an experimental musical instrument, which plays music by way of sea waves and tubes located underneath a set of large marble steps.

title: Carhenge is created
subtitle: Sculpture in Nebraska made from motor cars
fact: Carhenge is a replica of England's Stonehenge located near the city of Alliance, Nebraska, in the High Plains region of the United States.

title: Forever Marilyn is created
subtitle: Sculpture by John Seward Johnson II
fact: Forever Marilyn is a giant statue of Marilyn Monroe designed by Seward Johnson.

title: Monument to the laboratory mouse is created
subtitle: Sculpture in Novosibirsk, Siberia, Russia
fact: The Monument to the laboratory mouse is a sculpture in Novosibirsk' Akademgorodok, Siberia, Russia.

title: The Hand is created
subtitle: Sculpture in Punta del Este
fact: La Mano is a sculpture in Punta del Este by Chilean artist Mario Irarrázabal.

title: Flame of Liberty is created
subtitle: Monument in Paris, France
fact: The Flame of Liberty in Paris is a full-sized, gold-leaf-covered replica of the flame of the torch from New York City's Statue of Liberty.

title: Irish Hunger Memorial is created
subtitle: Memorial in New York City
fact: The Irish Hunger Memorial is a 0.5-acre (0.20 ha) park at the corner of Vesey Street and North End Avenue in the Battery Park City neighborhood of Manhattan in New York City.

title: Equestrian statue of Gattamelata is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Donatello
fact: The Equestrian Statue of Gattamelata is an Italian Renaissance sculpture by Donatello, dating from 1453, located on the Piazza del Santo in Padua, Italy.

title: 'Ain Ghazal Statues is created
subtitle: Early neolithic plaster statues found in the 1980s at Ain Ghazal in Jordan
fact: The ʿAin Ghazal statues are large-scale lime plaster and reed statues discovered at the archaeological site of ʿAin Ghazal in Amman, Jordan, dating back to approximately 9,000 years ago, from the Pre-Pottery Neolithic C period.

title: Khafre Enthroned is created
subtitle: Funerary statue in the Egyptian Museum in Cairo
fact: Khafre Enthroned is a Ka statue of the pharaoh Khafre, who reigned during the Fourth Dynasty of ancient Egypt.

title: Statue of Guan Yu (Jingzhou) is created
subtitle: Statue in Jingzhou, People's Republic of China
fact: The Guan Yu Statue was a large monument to deified Chinese military general Guan Yu that formerly stood in Jingzhou, China.

title: Madara Rider is created
subtitle: Early medieval rock relief
fact: The Madara Rider or Madara Horseman is a large early medieval rock relief carved on the Madara Plateau east of Shumen in northeastern Bulgaria, near the village of Madara.

title: Bronze Head from Ife is created
subtitle: Sculpture from Ife
fact: The Bronze Head from Ife, or Ife Head, is one of eighteen copper alloy sculptures that were unearthed in 1938 at Ife in Nigeria, the religious and royal centre of the Yoruba people.

title: World Clock is created
subtitle: Large clock in Berlin, Germany
fact: The World Clock, also known as the Urania World Clock, is a large turret-style world clock located in the public square of Alexanderplatz in Mitte, Berlin.

title: Children's Peace Monument is created
subtitle: Monument
fact: The Children's Peace Monument is a monument for peace to commemorate Sadako Sasaki and the thousands of child victims of the atomic bombing of Hiroshima.

title: Statue of Giordano Bruno is created
subtitle: Monument in the city of Rome
fact: The Monument to Giordano Bruno, created by the Grand Master Mason Ettore Ferrari, was erected in 1889 at Campo de' Fiori square in Rome, Italy, to commemorate the Italian philosopher Giordano Bruno, who was burned there in 1600.

title: Washington Monument is created
subtitle: Artwork by Enrico Causici and Robert Mills in Baltimore, United States
fact: The Washington Monument is the centerpiece of intersecting Mount Vernon Place and Washington Place, an urban square in the Mount Vernon-Belvedere neighborhood north of downtown Baltimore, Maryland.

title: The Sun Voyager is created
subtitle: Sculpture in Reykjavík by Jón Gunnar Árnason
fact: The Sun Voyager is a sculpture by Jón Gunnar Árnason, located next to the Sæbraut road in Reykjavík, Iceland.

title: Goethe–Schiller Monument is created
subtitle: Sculpture
fact: The original Goethe and Schiller Monument is in Weimar, Germany.

title: Capitoline Venus is created
subtitle: Statue
fact: The Capitoline Venus is a type of statue of Venus, specifically one of several Venus Pudica types, of which several examples exist.

title: Farnese Bull is created
subtitle: Sculpture
fact: The Farnese Bull, formerly in the Farnese collection in Rome, is a massive and elaborated Roman copy of a Hellenistic sculpture.

title: David with the head of Goliath is created
subtitle: Bronze statue by Andrea del Verrocchio, Museo nazionale del Bargello
fact: Andrea del Verrocchio's bronze statue of David was most likely made between 1473 and 1475.

title: Victorious Youth is created
subtitle: Ancient Greek bronze sculpture
fact: The Victorious Youth, also known as the Atleta di Fano, the Lisippo di Fano or the Getty Bronze, is a Greek bronze sculpture, made between 300 and 100 BCE, in the collections of the J.

title: Broken Chair is created
subtitle: Monumental sculpture in Geneva
fact: Broken Chair is a monumental sculpture in wood designed by Swiss artist Daniel Berset, and constructed by carpenter Louis Genève.

title: Monument to the Revolution of the people of Moslavina is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Dušan Džamonja
fact: Monument to the Revolution of the people of Moslavina or simply Monument to the Revolution is a World War II memorial sculpture by Dušan Džamonja, located in Podgarić, Berek municipality, Croatia.

title: Moon Museum is created
subtitle: Ceramic artwork
fact: Moon Museum is a small ceramic wafer three-quarters by one-half inch in size, containing artworks by six prominent artists from the late 1960s.

title: Jan Hus Memorial is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Ladislav Šaloun in Prague, Czech Republic
fact: The Jan Hus Memorial stands at one end of Old Town Square, Prague in the Czech Republic.

title: Gayer-Anderson cat is created
subtitle: Ancient Egyptian statue of a cat
fact: The Gayer-Anderson cat is an ancient Egyptian statue of a cat, which dates from the Late Period.

title: Flamingo is created
subtitle: Sculpture in Chicago, Illinois, by Alexander Calder
fact: Flamingo, a sculpture by American artist Alexander Calder, is a 53-foot-tall (16 m) stabile located in the Federal Plaza in front of the Kluczynski Federal Building in Chicago, Illinois, United States.

title: Prometheus is created
subtitle: Art Deco sculpture by Paul Manship
fact: Prometheus is a 1934 gilded, cast bronze sculpture by Paul Manship, located above the lower plaza at Rockefeller Center in Manhattan, New York City.

title: Pediments of the Parthenon is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Phidias
fact: The pediments of the Parthenon are the two sets of statues in Pentelic marble originally located as the pedimental sculpture on the east and west facades of the Parthenon on the Acropolis of Athens.

title: Statue of Bruce Lee is created
subtitle: Statue in Hong Kong
fact: The Bruce Lee statue in Hong Kong is a bronze memorial statue of the martial artist Bruce Lee, who died on 20 July 1973 at the age of 32, created by sculptor Cao Chong-en, and located on the Avenue of Stars attraction near the waterfront at Tsim Sha Tsui.

title: Tomb of Pope Alexander VII is created
subtitle: Funerary monument by Gianlorenzo Bernini
fact: The Tomb of Pope Alexander VII is a sculptural monument designed and partially executed by the Italian artist Gian Lorenzo Bernini.

title: Great Sphinx of Tanis is created
subtitle: Granite sphinx in the collections of the Louvre
fact: The Great Sphinx of Tanis is an ancient Egyptian pink granite sculpture of a sphinx.

title: Equestrian statue of Joan of Arc is created
subtitle: Statue by Emmanuel Frémiet in Paris, France
fact: Jeanne d'Arc is an 1874 French gilded bronze equestrian sculpture of Joan of Arc by Emmanuel Frémiet.

title: Saint Cecilia is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Stefano Maderno
fact: Saint Cecilia is a marble sculpture by the Italian sculptor Stefano Maderno, completed c. 1600 for Santa Cecilia in Trastevere in Rome, Italy.

title: Alma Mater is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Daniel Chester French
fact: Alma Mater is a bronze sculpture by Daniel Chester French which is located on the steps of the Low Memorial Library on the campus of Columbia University, in the Morningside Heights neighborhood of Manhattan, New York City.

title: Coatlicue statue is created
subtitle: 2.52 Metre (8.3 ft) tall andesite statue by an unidentified Mexica artist
fact: The Coatlicue statue is one of the most famous surviving Aztec sculptures.

title: Apoxyomenos is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Lysippos
fact: Apoxyomenos is one of the conventional subjects of ancient Greek votive sculpture; it represents an athlete, caught in the familiar act of scraping sweat and dust from his body with the small curved instrument that the Greeks called a stlengis and the Romans a strigil.

title: Crouching Venus is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Doidalsa
fact: The Crouching Venus is a Hellenistic model of Venus surprised at her bath.

title: Flying Horse of Gansu is created
subtitle: Chinese bronze sculpture
fact: The Flying Horse of Gansu, also known as the Bronze Running Horse (銅奔馬) or the Galloping Horse Treading on a Flying Swallow (馬踏飛燕), is a Chinese bronze sculpture from circa the 2nd century CE.

title: Statue of Oliver Cromwell is created
subtitle: Statue in Westminster, London, by Hamo Thornycroft
fact: A statue of Oliver Cromwell stands outside the House of Commons of the United Kingdom in Westminster, London.

title: Gemma Augustea is created
subtitle: Cameo of the Julio-Claudians
fact: The Gemma Augustea is an ancient Roman low-relief cameo engraved gem cut from a double-layered Arabian onyx stone.

title: Ram in a Thicket is created
subtitle: Sculptures excavated in Ur, in southern Iraq
fact: The Ram in a Thicket is a pair of figures excavated at Ur, which date from about 2600–2400 BC.

title: Yazılıkaya is created
subtitle: Sanctuary of Hattusa, the capital city of the Hittite Empire
fact: Yazılıkaya was a sanctuary of Hattusa, the capital city of the Hittite Empire, today in the Çorum Province, Turkey.

title: Venus of Moravany is created
subtitle: Paleolithic Venus figurine
fact: The Venus of Moravany is a small prehistoric Venus figurine discovered in Slovakia in the early 20th century.

title: Monument to the Republic is created
subtitle: Statue at the center of the Place de la République, Paris
fact: The Monument à la République, also called Statue de la République, is a Monumental sculpture, made by the sculptor Léopold Morice.

title: Venus of Galgenberg is created
subtitle: Sculpture
fact: The Venus of Galgenberg is a Venus figurine of the Aurignacian era, dated about 36,000 years ago.

title: Crucifix is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Michelangelo
fact: Two different crucifixes, or strictly, wooden corpus sculptures for crucifixes, are attributed to the High Renaissance master Michelangelo, although neither is universally accepted as his.

title: Stalin Monument is created
subtitle: Destroyed statue, originally in Budapest
fact: The Stalin Monument was a statue of Joseph Stalin in Budapest, Hungary.

title: Pasquino is created
subtitle: One of the six talking statues of Rome
fact: Pasquino or Pasquin is the name used by Romans since the early modern period to describe a battered Hellenistic-style statue perhaps dating to the third century BC, which was unearthed in the Parione district of Rome in the fifteenth century.

title: Object is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Méret Oppenheim
fact: Object, lit.

title: Fuente del Ángel Caído is created
subtitle: Fountain in Madrid
fact: The Fuente del Ángel Caído is a fountain located in the Buen Retiro Park in Madrid, Spain.

title: Madonna of the Stair is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Michelangelo
fact: The Madonna of the Stairs is a relief sculpture by Michelangelo in the Casa Buonarroti, Florence.

title: Sarcophagus of Junius Bassus is created
subtitle: Early Christian Sarcophagus used by Junius Bassus
fact: The Sarcophagus of Junius Bassus is a marble Early Christian sarcophagus used for the burial of Junius Bassus, who died in 359.

title: Hand of Irulegi is created
subtitle: Vasconic archaeological artifact from 1st century BC
fact: The Hand of Irulegi is a late Iron Age archaeological artifact unearthed in 2021 during excavations in the archaeological site of Irulegi (Navarre), next to the medieval castle of Irulegi, located in the municipality of Aranguren, Spain.

title: Taddei Tondo is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Michelangelo
fact: The Taddei Tondo or The Virgin and Child with the Infant St.

title: Borghese Gladiator is created
subtitle: Hellenistic sculpture
fact: The Borghese Gladiator is a Hellenistic life-size marble sculpture portraying a swordsman, created at Ephesus about 100 BC, now on display at the Louvre.

title: Saint Ludovica Albertoni is created
subtitle: Sculpture / artwork by Gianlorenzo Bernini
fact: Blessed Ludovica Albertoni is a funerary monument by the Italian Baroque artist Gian Lorenzo Bernini.

title: Robert Edward Lee sculpture is created
subtitle: Former public statue in Charlottesville, Virginia
fact: The Robert E.

title: Ludovisi Ares is created
subtitle: Roman sculpture from second century Common Era
fact: The Ludovisi Ares is an Antonine Roman marble sculpture of Ares, a fine 2nd-century copy of a late 4th-century BCE Greek original, associated with Scopas or Lysippus: thus the Roman god of war receives his Greek name, Ares.

title: Battle of the Centaurs is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Michelangelo
fact: Battle of the Centaurs is a relief sculpture by the Italian Renaissance artist Michelangelo, created around 1492.

title: Ludovisi Battle sarcophagus is created
subtitle: Ancient Roman sarcophagus
fact: The Ludovisi Battle sarcophagus or "Great" Ludovisi sarcophagus is an ancient Roman sarcophagus dating to around AD 250–260, found in 1621 in the Vigna Bernusconi, a tomb near the Porta Tiburtina.

title: Liver of Piacenza is created
subtitle: Etruscan artifact
fact: The Liver of Piacenza is an Etruscan artifact found in a field on September 26, 1877, near Gossolengo, in the province of Piacenza, Italy, now kept in the Municipal Museum of Piacenza, in the Palazzo Farnese.

title: Zbruch Idol is created
subtitle: Slavic religious sculpture
fact: The Zbruch Idol, Sviatovid is a 9th-century limestone sculpture idol, and one of the few monuments of pre-Christian Slavic beliefs.

title: Well of Moses is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Claus Sluter
fact: The Well of Moses is a monumental sculpture recognised as the masterpiece of the Dutch artist Claus Sluter (1340–1405–06), assisted by his nephew Claus de Werve.

title: B of the Bang is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Thomas Heatherwick
fact: B of the Bang was a sculpture by Thomas Heatherwick next to the City of Manchester Stadium in east Manchester, England, commissioned to mark the 2002 Commonwealth Games.

title: Venus of Monruz is created
subtitle: Sculpture
fact: The Venus of Monruz is a Venus figurine of the late Upper Paleolithic, or the beginning Epipaleolithic, dating to the end of the Magdalenian, some 11,000 years ago.

title: Victims of Communism Memorial is created
subtitle: Artwork
fact: The Victims of Communism Memorial is a memorial in Washington, D.C. located at the intersection of Massachusetts and New Jersey Avenues and G Street, NW, two blocks from Washington Union Station within view of the U.S.

title: Het Zinneke is created
subtitle: Bronze sculpture by Tom Frantzen in Brussels, Belgium
fact: Het Zinneke, sometimes called Zinneke Pis by analogy with Manneken Pis, is a bronze sculpture in central Brussels, Belgium, erected in 1999.

title: Truth is created
subtitle: Sculpture in the Galleria Borghese, Rome, Italy, by Gian Lorenzo Bernini
fact: Truth Unveiled by Time is a marble sculpture by Italian artist Gian Lorenzo Bernini, one of the foremost sculptors of the Italian Baroque.

title: Statue of Antinous is created
subtitle: Statue at the Delphi museum
fact: The Statue of Antinous at Delphi is an ancient statue that was found during excavations in Delphi.

title: Mask of Warka is created
subtitle: Mask possibly depicting Inanna
fact: The Mask of Warka, also known as the Lady of Uruk, dating from 3100 BC, is one of the earliest known representations of the human face.

title: Piraeus Athena is created
subtitle: Statue
fact: The Piraeus Athena is a Greek bronze statue dated to the fourth century BCE.

title: The Orator is created
subtitle: Roman-Etruscan bronze sculpture
fact: The Orator, also known as L'Arringatore (Italian), Aule Meteli (Etruscan) or Aulus Metellus (Latin), is an Etruscan bronze sculpture from the late second or the early first century BC.

title: Capitoline Brutus is created
subtitle: Ancient Roman bronze bust
fact: The Capitoline Brutus is an ancient Roman bronze bust that is traditionally, but probably wrongly, thought to be an imagined portrait of the Roman consul Lucius Junius Brutus.

title: Angel is created
subtitle: Marble statue by Michelangelo
fact: The statue of an Angel (1494–1495) was created by Michelangelo out of marble.

title: Memorial to Homosexuals Persecuted Under Nazism in Berlin is created
subtitle: Memorial in Berlin, Germany
fact: The Memorial to Homosexuals persecuted under Nazism in Berlin was opened on 27 May 2008.

title: Hercules of the Forum Boarium is created
subtitle: Gilded bronze statues of Hercules at the Capitoline Museums, Rome, Italy
fact: Hercules of the Forum Boarium is an ancient Roman gilded bronze statue depicting Hercules that was originally located near the Forum Boarium.

title: Karl Marx Monument is created
subtitle: Monument in Chemnitz
fact: The Karl Marx Monument is a 7.10-metre-tall (23-foot-4-inch) stylized head of Karl Marx in Chemnitz, Germany.

title: Altarpiece of Veit Stoss is created
subtitle: Largest Gothic altarpiece, national treasure of Poland
fact: The Altarpiece by Veit Stoss, also St.

title: Barberini ivory is created
subtitle: Relief
fact: The Barberini ivory is a Byzantine ivory leaf from an imperial diptych dating from Late Antiquity, now in the Louvre in Paris.

title: Sultanganj Buddha is created
subtitle: Largest copper Buddha statue of the world
fact: The Sultanganj Buddha is a Gupta–Pala transitional period sculpture, the largest substantially complete copper Buddha figure known from the time.

title: Antikythera Ephebe is created
subtitle: Greek bronze statue
fact: The Antikythera Ephebe, registered as Bronze statue of a youth in the museum collections, is a Greek bronze statue of a young man of languorous grace that was found in 1900 by sponge-divers in the area of the ancient Antikythera shipwreck off the island of Antikythera, Greece.

title: Namara inscription is created
subtitle: Ancient arabic inscription
fact: The Namara inscription is a 4th century inscription in the Arabic language, making it one of the earliest.

title: Statue of Franz Kafka is created
subtitle: Statue in Prague on the border of the Old Town and Josefov
fact: A statue of Franz Kafka by artist Jaroslav Róna was installed on Vězeňská street in the Jewish Quarter of Prague, Czech Republic in December 2003.

title: Statue of Amy Winehouse is created
subtitle: Statue in Camden Town, London
fact: A bronze statue of the British singer Amy Winehouse is located at the Stables Market in Camden Town, in north London.

title: Apollo of Veii is created
subtitle: Late 6th century BC painted terracotta Etruscan statue of Apollo
fact: The Apollo of Veii is a life-size painted terracotta Etruscan statue of Aplu (Apollo), designed to be placed at the highest part of a temple.

title: Monument to the Women of World War II is created
subtitle: British national war memorial situated on Whitehall in London, UK
fact: The Monument to the Women of World War II is a British national war memorial situated on Whitehall in London next to the Cenotaph at the end of Downing Street.

title: Neptune and Triton is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Gian Lorenzo Bernini, Victoria and Albert Museum
fact: Neptune and Triton is an early sculpture by the Italian artist Gian Lorenzo Bernini.

title: Jockey of Artemision is created
subtitle: Hellenistic bronze statue
fact: The Jockey of Artemision is a large Hellenistic bronze statue of a young boy riding a horse, dated to around 150–140 BC.

title: James A. Garfield Monument is created
subtitle: Statue in Washington, D.C., by John Quincy Adams Ward
fact: The James A.

title: Diadumenos is created
subtitle: Lost original bronze sculpture by Polykleitos the Elder
fact: The Diadumenos, together with the Doryphoros, are two of the most famous figural types of the sculptor Polyclitus, forming a basic pattern of Ancient Greek sculpture that all present strictly idealized representations of young male athletes in a convincingly naturalistic manner.

title: The Vision of Constantine is created
subtitle: Statue by Gian Lorenzo Bernini in St. Peter's Basilica, Vatican City
fact: The Vision of Constantine is an equestrian sculpture by the Italian artist Gian Lorenzo Bernini, located in the Scala Regia by St.

title: Great Cameo of France is created
subtitle: Sculpture
fact: The Great Cameo of France is a five-layered sardonyx Imperial Roman cameo of either about 23 AD, or 50–54 AD.

title: Chollima Statue is created
subtitle: Monument in Pyongyang, North Korea
fact: The Chollima Statue (Korean: 천리마동상) is a monument on Mansu Hill in Pyongyang, North Korea.

title: The Eternal Springtime is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Auguste Rodin
fact: Eternal Springtime is a c. 1884 sculpture by the French artist Auguste Rodin, depicting a pair of lovers.

title: Lanzón is created
subtitle: Sacred monolith in Chavin temple, Peru
fact: The Lanzón is a granite stela that is associated with the Chavín culture.

title: Brno astronomical clock is created
subtitle: Monument in Brno, Czech Republic
fact: The Brno astronomical clock is a black stone monument in Brno, Czech Republic.

title: Fontaine Bartholdi is created
subtitle: Fountain in Lyon, France
fact: The Fontaine Bartholdi is a fountain sculpted by Frédéric Auguste Bartholdi and realised in 1889 by Gaget & Gautier.

title: Adam Mickiewicz Monument, Kraków is created
subtitle: Monument in Poland
fact: Adam Mickiewicz Monument, a bronze monument located in the Main Market Square in the Old Town of Kraków, Poland.

title: Bamberg Horseman is created
subtitle: Sculpture in the cathedral of Bamberg, Germany
fact: The Bamberg Horseman is an early 13th-century stone equestrian statue by an anonymous medieval sculptor in the cathedral of Bamberg, Germany.

title: Golden Madonna of Essen is created
subtitle: Sculpture of the Virgin Mary and the infant Jesus in Essen Cathedral, Germany
fact: The Golden Mother of Good Counsel of Essen is the eldest Roman Catholic sculpture of the Blessed Virgin Mary and the Child Jesus.

title: Sphinx of Naxos is created
subtitle: Sculpture
fact: The Sphinx of Naxos, also Sphinx of the Naxians, now in the Archaeological Museum of Delphi, is a 2.22-meter-tall (7.3 ft) marble statue of a sphinx, a mythical creature with the head of a woman, the chest and wings composed of the impressive feathers of a prey bird turned upward, and the body of a lioness.

title: Ares Borghese is created
subtitle: Sculpture by anonymous
fact: The Ares Borghese is a Roman marble statue of the imperial era.

title: National Women's Monument is created
subtitle: Monument in Bloemfontein, South Africa
fact: The National Women's Monument in Bloemfontein, South Africa, is a monument commemorating the roughly 27,000 Boer women and children who died in British concentration camps during the Second Boer War.

title: Traffic Light tree is created
subtitle: Sculpture by Pierre Vivant
fact: Traffic Light Tree is a public sculpture in between Poplar and Blackwall, London, England, created by the French sculptor Pierre Vivant following a competition run by the Public Art Commissions Agency for the London Docklands Development Corporation under their Public Art programme.

title: Moschophoros is created
subtitle: Ancient sculpture from the Acropolis of Athens
fact: Moschophoros is an ancient Greek statue of the Archaic period, also known in English as The Calf Bearer.

title: Benjamin Franklin National Memorial is created
subtitle: Historic site in Pennsylvania, U.S
fact: The Benjamin Franklin National Memorial, located in the rotunda of the Franklin Institute science museum in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, features a large statue of a seated Benjamin Franklin, American writer, inventor, statesman, and Founding Father.

title: Lady of Baza is created
subtitle: Iberian sculpture
fact: The Lady of Baza is a famous example of Iberian sculpture by the Bastetani.

title: The Destroyed City is created
subtitle: Public artwork by Ossip Zadkine in Rotterdam, the Netherlands
fact: The Destroyed City is a bronze memorial sculpture in the Dutch city of Rotterdam.

title: Via Labicana Augustus is created
subtitle: Sculpture
fact: The Via Labicana Augustus is a marble statue of the Roman emperor Augustus as Pontifex Maximus depicted in religious ceremony.
